texto: It's important that you don't continue to ignore or accept rages. Realize that extreme rage directed at you or your children is verbal and emotional abuse. Even if you think you can handle it, over time it can erode your self-esteem and poison the relationship. Seek support immediately.
explicacao: Citação de Randi Kreger sobre limites e escolhas conscientes. “It's important that you don't continue to ignore or accept rages. Realize that extreme rage direc…” articula autoestima, abuse, anger em registro de autoestima, com relevância para contextos como anger e domestic-violence.

frase_original: It's important that you don't continue to ignore or accept rages. Realize that extreme rage directed at you or your children is verbal and emotional abuse. Even if you think you can handle it, over time it can erode your self-esteem and poison the relationship. Seek support immediately.

texto: Children who experience abuse also learn to deny pain and chaos or accept them as normal and proper. They learn that their feelings were wrong or didn't matter. They learn to focus on immediate survival - on not getting abused, and miss out on important developmental stages. As a result, they have problems developing their own identities.
explicacao: Orientação direta de Randi Kreger sobre temas ligados a familia. A afirmação “Children who experience abuse also learn to deny pain and chaos or accept them as normal and prop…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a familia, abuse, child-abuse, útil em momentos de child-abuse.
